title: Mac and Charlie Die (1)

jokes.text: Yeah, you're asking us to predict the future, Dee. How can I predict the future?
jokes.setup: Dee asks what they're doing tonight
jokes.punchline: Mac treats it as an impossible metaphysical request
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac logic - taking a simple question to absurd extremes with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Dee, you tried this shit 30 years ago when that show first came on the air.
jokes.setup: Dee wants to go to a martini bar after watching Sex and the City
jokes.punchline: Charlie implies Dee is impossibly old
jokes.explanation: Sharp math joke that doubles as an insult - Sex and the City wasn't even close to 30 years old

jokes.text: I did not poop the bed!
jokes.punchline: Sudden explosive denial about bed-pooping
jokes.explanation: Perfect cold open escalation - goes from mundane to absurd instantly, establishes the episode premise

jokes.text: Dee, when we said we can't predict the future, how could we possibly have seen something this awesome coming?
jokes.setup: Mac's earlier complaint about predicting the future
jokes.punchline: Now he's excited about the unpredictable poop mystery
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Mac's shifting priorities - dismissive of plans but obsessed with poop mystery

jokes.text: Wait a minute, so this happened two nights in a row?
jokes.punchline: Dennis realizes the horror has repeated
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - making the situation worse by revealing it's a pattern.

jokes.text: So we threw the first piece of poop out 'cause big deal, whatever. Accidents happen, right?
jokes.punchline: Treating bed-pooping as a minor inconvenience that 'accidents happen'
jokes.explanation: Charlie's casual dismissal of a disgusting situation is perfectly in character and absurdly understated.

jokes.text: Hey, girlfriend! Oh, my God, you scared me! Did I? My bad.
jokes.setup: Dee trying to be casual and friendly
jokes.punchline: She was literally stalking from behind a car
jokes.explanation: Dee's oblivious creepiness - acting like stalking behavior is normal

jokes.text: Why did you jump out from behind a car? Were you stalking me?
jokes.punchline: Waitress immediately identifies predatory behavior
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Dee's creepy desperation, Waitress cuts right through the fake friendliness.

jokes.text: That's the way girls are. We're catty. We backstab and talk about each other behind our backs and stalk.
jokes.punchline: Casually includes stalking as normal girl behavior
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that reveals Dee's warped understanding of relationships

jokes.text: Frederick is your cat and he died three weeks ago.
jokes.setup: Dee claiming to have friends, naming Frederick
jokes.punchline: Frederick was a dead cat
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical reveal of how pathetic the situation is

jokes.text: We want you to examine our poopie.
jokes.setup: Approaching what appears to be a scientist
jokes.punchline: Childish term 'poopie' for serious forensic request
jokes.explanation: Infantile language contrasts with serious scientific pretensions

jokes.text: You've been sleeping in a giant dress shirt. It probably just fell out the bottom of it.
jokes.setup: Frank defending himself against poop accusations
jokes.punchline: Charlie's nightwear is so inappropriate poop could just fall out
jokes.explanation: Vivid visual comedy - Frank's matter-of-fact description of poop logistics

jokes.text: You want me to examine a plastic container full of feces?
jokes.punchline: Scientist's clinical but horrified restatement
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction that emphasizes the absurdity of their request.

jokes.text: You want me to examine a plastic container full of feces?
jokes.setup: Their casual request for poop analysis
jokes.punchline: The scientist's horrified realization of what they're asking
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction to their insane request, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: You just missed out on an extraordinary adventure, pal.
jokes.setup: Scientist refuses to examine their feces
jokes.punchline: Mac frames poop analysis as a thrilling opportunity
jokes.explanation: Mac's delusional framing of gross situation as adventure

jokes.text: You got turd? Yeah, we got a turd. I'll take a look at it.
jokes.punchline: Immediate enthusiasm for examining feces
jokes.explanation: Casual drug-deal tone applied to poop examination

jokes.text: Whoever it was seems to have been eating newspaper.
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact revelation about newspaper consumption
jokes.explanation: Casual delivery of disturbing dietary information

jokes.text: This is wolf hair. Wolf air. Also inconclusive.
jokes.punchline: Wolf hair in poop, plus Charlie's mishearing
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity - wolf hair consumption treated as normal, plus Charlie's innocent mishearing

jokes.text: There is so much wolf hair in our apartment right now, man.
jokes.punchline: Charlie treats having wolf hair everywhere as a minor inconvenience
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Charlie's casual acceptance of having wolf hair 'in our apartment right now' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Both of you are eating wolf? Have you tried it before?
jokes.punchline: Scientist asking if they've tried eating wolf like it's cuisine
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where even the fake scientist gets drawn into their absurd world.

jokes.text: No, I'm an Econ major. I just wanted to look at some poop.
jokes.setup: Characters assume he's a qualified scientist
jokes.punchline: He's just a student with scatological curiosity
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection payoff - casual honesty about weird motivation

jokes.text: I'm gonna take off my bra, blast my nips.
jokes.punchline: Artemis's crude and direct approach
jokes.explanation: Classic Artemis - immediately going to extreme sexual tactics

jokes.text: Actually, make mine a water. Water? We're in a bar. Yeah, well, I have a drinking problem.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of alcoholism in social setting
jokes.explanation: Unexpected honesty about serious issue in casual context

jokes.text: Yeah, well, I have a drinking problem.
jokes.setup: Waitress orders water at a bar
jokes.punchline: Casually announces alcoholism as explanation
jokes.explanation: Good character development delivered with perfect casual timing.

jokes.text: Name's Artemis. I have a bleached asshole.
jokes.punchline: Immediate oversharing of intimate grooming details
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artemis moment - immediate escalation to maximum inappropriate oversharing. Becomes iconic line.

jokes.text: Do we come to your house and tell you how to sleep?
jokes.setup: Dennis suggests they need two beds
jokes.punchline: Frank gets defensive about their disgusting sleeping arrangement
jokes.explanation: Frank treating bed-sharing with poop incidents as a valid lifestyle choice is absurdly defensive.

jokes.text: Dennis on his bed made for kings with his toilet made out of gold.
jokes.punchline: Frank's resentful exaggeration of Dennis's lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Frank's bitter class resentment expressed through bathroom hyperbole

jokes.text: Charlie, we sleep ass to ass, you know that.
jokes.punchline: Frank's matter-of-fact description of intimate arrangement
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual delivery of deeply uncomfortable sleeping arrangement details

jokes.text: It smells like some kind of spice blend or pesticide, or some other kind of sandy poison or something.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dire descriptions of Charlie and Frank's living conditions
jokes.explanation: Dennis's disgusted catalog of apartment hazards escalates comically

jokes.text: I'll hold your boobs a little better.
jokes.setup: Dee telling drunk Waitress she should hold her booze better
jokes.punchline: Drunk mishearing creates inappropriate comeback
jokes.explanation: Basic drunk mishearing joke - functional but not sophisticated

jokes.text: How much? 700. 700?
jokes.punchline: Outrageous price reveal
jokes.explanation: Standard price shock joke, but sets up the credit card decline sequence.

jokes.text: Man, I am excited to bust my dogs out in these tonight.
jokes.punchline: Crude expression about displaying her feet
jokes.explanation: Dee's attempt at sexy talk comes out awkward and unappetizing

jokes.text: Frank, I will jam that poop right back up your ass where it came from.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's graphic threat of fecal reversal
jokes.explanation: Charlie's innocent delivery of shockingly graphic threat escalates the conflict perfectly

jokes.text: we should not be threatening each other with jamming feces into each other's assholes, okay?
jokes.punchline: Mac's attempt at reasonable mediation using clinical language
jokes.explanation: Mac's straight-man response treats the absurd threat as a reasonable mediation issue

jokes.text: He was turtle-heading. Turtle-heading! No, there was no turtles anywhere.
jokes.setup: Mac using crude slang for almost defecating
jokes.punchline: Charlie's literal interpretation about actual turtles
jokes.explanation: Classic Charlie misunderstanding of crude slang

jokes.text: I'm gonna go get more wasted, and then I'm gonna bang some rim, dude.
jokes.setup: Artemis frustrated with the evening's direction
jokes.punchline: Crude declaration of sexual intentions
jokes.explanation: Artemis's typically crude response to frustration

jokes.text: You have a bad attitude when you're drinking, you huge-footed slut.
jokes.punchline: Artemis fires back with specific body-shaming
jokes.explanation: Artemis calling back to Dee's large feet while insulting the wrong person adds to the chaos.

jokes.text: It's almost as if you can smell it. Oh, God...
jokes.setup: Pretentious art analysis of what's actually poop
jokes.punchline: Moment of realization about what they're actually examining
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff - art pretension meets literal reality

jokes.text: Well... I think it's crap. Crap...
jokes.punchline: Double meaning - both art criticism and literal description
jokes.explanation: Perfect pun that works on multiple levels - art criticism and literal truth

jokes.text: Four turds, five suspects, so many, many nameless victims.
jokes.punchline: Treating poop mystery like serious crime drama
jokes.explanation: Charlie's perfect parody of noir detective tropes applied to absurd situation

jokes.text: Tall order for such a short man.
jokes.punchline: Physical joke about Frank's height affecting poop frequency
jokes.explanation: Charlie's detective voice maintains character specificity with height joke

jokes.text: And like a small brown snail... it crept to the middle between Charlie and Frank.
jokes.setup: Charlie's detective theory about poop physics
jokes.punchline: Poetic metaphor for poop movement across a slanted futon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist poetry - Charlie's beautiful metaphor for disgusting physics

jokes.text: The lonely, sad, slutty, bitchy whore.
jokes.setup: Charlie listing Dee's attributes as suspect
jokes.punchline: Escalating chain of increasingly harsh descriptors
jokes.explanation: Charlie's detective voice maintains the escalating insult structure

jokes.text: Thunder of the... chocolate variety.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's dramatic pause before crude euphemism
jokes.explanation: Charlie's dramatic detective voice with juvenile euphemism creates perfect contrast

jokes.text: Known the world over for his ability to replicate any man's stool, Cricket came back and committed fecal forgery.
jokes.punchline: Cricket as professional poop mimic, 'fecal forgery' as crime
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Cricket's absurd specialization and the term 'fecal forgery'

jokes.text: A turd merger... fused together by fear and hatred... and hair.
jokes.setup: Dennis and Mac's poops rolling together
jokes.punchline: Corporate merger language for poop physics plus wolf hair callback
jokes.explanation: Charlie applies business terminology to scatological physics with callback

jokes.text: I did it. I did 'em all. I did all the poops. I even did one, while she was rappin us, right here.
jokes.punchline: Artemis's simple confession plus immediate demonstration
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - elaborate theories deflated by simple truth and immediate escalation

jokes.text: Because poop is funny.
jokes.setup: Everyone asking why Artemis pooped everywhere
jokes.punchline: Simple, undeniable truth about comedy
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-commentary - Artemis states the fundamental truth about the entire episode

jokes.text: I guess poop is very funny.
jokes.punchline: Charlie's philosophical acceptance of the situation
jokes.explanation: Charlie's resigned acceptance provides a nice bookend to his elaborate detective work.

tmdb_overview: Part 1 of 2. Mac and Charlie fake their deaths to avoid the wrath of Mac's father who was released from prison and is out for vengeance.
